name: Puerto de Tollos and Font Roja Sanctuary Loop
name_original: Ascenso al puerto de Tollos y el Santuario de la Font Roja — Circular desde Alcoi
description: This difficult 58.7-mile road cycling route from Alcoi climbs to Puerto de Tollos and the Font Roja Sanctuary, offering panoramic views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alcoi are set within a diverse natural landscape, characterized by mountainous terrain, dense pine and Mediterranean forests, and significant natural parks like Sierra Mariola and Font Roja. The region features numerous natural springs, rivers, and dramatic rock formations, offering varied elevation profiles for road cyclists. Challenging ascents, including the Font Roja climb and Xorret de Catí, are prominent, alongside more accessible greenways. Best road cycling routes around Alcoi The most popular road cycling route is Port de Tudons Loop from Alcoy, a 36.9 miles (59.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Tollos and Font Roja Sanctuary Loop, a difficult 58.7 miles (94.4 km) path. This route traverses through the Font Roja Natural Park, offering shaded cycling and panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Bridge over the Sella River – El Collao Pass (890 m) loop from Alcoi, a 53.3 miles (85.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with significant climbs, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Alcoi is defined by mountainous terrain, natural parks, and challenging climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from demanding ascents to more moderate loops. The routes in Alcoi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcoi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alcoi?
A: Alcoi offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 50 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 3 easy, 23 moderate, and 24 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Alcoi?
A: Road cycling in Alcoi is characterized by diverse mountainous terrain, featuring significant elevation changes. You'll encounter dense pine and Mediterranean forests, natural springs, rivers like the Vinalopó and Serpis, and dramatic rock formations. The region is home to two major natural parks, Sierra Mariola and Font Roja, which contribute to the varied landscapes and challenging climbs.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in Alcoi?
A: Absolutely. Alcoi is renowned for its demanding ascents, making it a prime destination for experienced road cyclists. Notable climbs include the classic &lt;strong&gt;Font Roja climb&lt;/strong&gt; with its hairpin bends, and the formidable &lt;strong&gt;Xorret de Catí&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Guixop&lt;/strong&gt;, known for their very steep gradients. Xorret de Catí has even been featured in the Vuelta cycling race.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling options in Alcoi?
A: Yes, for easier and family-friendly cycling, the &lt;strong&gt;Alcoy Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; is an excellent choice. This popular route follows an old railway line, stretching 10 kilometers one-way with both dirt and asphalted sections. It features 11 illuminated tunnels and 3 viaducts, offering a unique and safe cycling experience. The Cocentaina Greenway also connects to Alcoi, providing additional accessible paths.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Alcoi?
A: Many road cycling routes around Alcoi are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location while exploring the region's natural beauty. Examples include the "Sierra Mariola Circle" and loops incorporating the Font Roja Sanctuary or Port de Tudons, offering panoramic views and immersive experiences.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Alcoi?
A: The routes often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You can encounter features like the extensive Sierra Mariola Natural Park with peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit&lt;/a&gt;, and the well-preserved Mediterranean forests of Font Roja Natural Park. Some routes might also lead you past natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3485518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2546615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cava Gran de Agres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest accessible by road bike?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Alcoi offers cultural and historical attractions. The city's historic center is a declared Historic-Artistic Site, with notable places like the Plaza de España and the Calderón theater. An industrial route also allows exploration of the city's heritage along the Riquer and Molinar rivers. While not directly on all road cycling routes, these points are easily accessible from Alcoi.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Alcoi?
A: The road cycling experience in Alcoi is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the stunning natural parks like Sierra Mariola and Font Roja. The well-maintained road surfaces and the variety of routes for all levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is parking available near road cycling trailheads in Alcoi?
A: Alcoi is generally cyclist-friendly, and you can find parking options within the city and near popular starting points for routes. Many accommodations also offer secure garages for bikes, making it convenient for cyclists to store their equipment safely.

Q: What is a recommended moderate road cycling route in Alcoi?
A: For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14666612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cocentaina Castle – View of Alcoi loop from Alcoi&lt;/a&gt;. This 53.2-mile (85.8 km) route offers varied landscapes and takes approximately 4 hours 44 minutes to complete, providing a balanced experience without the extreme difficulty of the most challenging climbs.

Q: What is a recommended difficult road cycling route in Alcoi?
A: A highly recommended difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32137119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Sella River – El Collao Pass (890 m) loop from Alcoi&lt;/a&gt;. This 53.3-mile (85.8 km) trail features significant climbs and varied landscapes, typically completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes, offering a substantial challenge for experienced riders.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes directly on every route might vary, Alcoi and the surrounding towns offer amenities for cyclists, including cafes and pubs where you can refuel. It's always a good idea to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer routes through natural parks, to ensure you have access to refreshments.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcoi?
A: Alcoi offers good road cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially in Font Roja Natural Park when the Mediterranean mixed forest transforms with ochre and reddish hues. Summers can be hot, so early morning rides are recommended, while winters are generally mild, allowing for year-round cycling.
tips: Picturesque mountain port in the interior of the province of Alicante. With 6% ramps and a surface in good condition, it is a climb to enjoy the journey. It can be somewhat more demanding in the North-South direction, so if you want an extra effort, do it in this direction.

It is located within the Carrascar de la Font Roja natural park. The sanctuary is not only a religious site, but also a viewpoint with incredible panoramic views of the L'Alcoia region and a place from which tours to visit the park begin.

The access road is in very good condition and has not very demanding slopes, which allow you to enjoy the views.

Near the sanctuary is the Red Fountain where you can refill water and, a few meters away, a recreational area with tables, benches and bathrooms. A very nice place for the summer, since the thick grove of holm oaks gives a lot of shade.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult and is categorized as a "Hard road ride." It demands very good fitness due to its significant length of 94.4 kilometers and an impressive 1,800 meters of elevation gain. It's best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and road surface like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain characteristic of mountainous regions. The Puerto de Tollos section has ramps with an average gradient of 6%, with a generally good road surface. The climb towards the Font Roja Sanctuary has a very good paved surface throughout, including some hairpin bends with steep corners, though these steep sections are not sustained. All surfaces are suitable for road cycling.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse landscapes, including a beautiful climb through a pine forest towards the Puerto de Tollos. The route also takes you through the Font Roja Natural Park, an excellent example of a mixed Mediterranean forest with holm oaks, Valencian oaks, maples, ashes, and yews. The Font Roja Sanctuary itself is a significant landmark.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, while some forest sections may limit panoramic views, the Font Roja Sanctuary provides incredible panoramic views of the surrounding area. It serves as a popular viewpoint within the natural park.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Alcoi?
faqs.answer: The route is circular from Alcoi, so you can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride. Specific parking areas for cyclists are usually available near the main access points or town centers. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and more pleasant for demanding rides. Summer can be very hot, especially during the climbs, and winter might bring colder temperatures at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route, especially within the Font Roja Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads or designated forest tracks within the Parc Natural del Carrascar de la Font Roja. However, cycling is permitted on forest tracks but prohibited on trails or cross-country within the park. Always respect park regulations and signage.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route, and bringing dogs along for such a long and strenuous ride is generally not recommended. While dogs might be allowed in certain areas of the natural park on a leash, the nature of this specific road cycling tour makes it unsuitable for pets.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the CIMA AT09 Font Roja, the Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós, and El Camí IT5TR4 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana. It also touches upon the GR 7: Andorra - Strait of Gibraltar and the Via Verda d'Alcoi.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water and nutrition, appropriate cycling gear including a helmet, spare tubes/repair kit, and a phone for emergencies. Sunscreen and layers of clothing are also recommended due to varying elevations and potential weather changes.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, Alcoi at the start/end offers various facilities. The Font Roja Sanctuary area may have limited facilities. It's always wise to carry enough supplies and plan stops in towns or villages along the way for refreshments and restrooms.
